articles.title: 'One little half sentence' & ridiculous SC ruling on tariffs could have saved US $159 billion, says Trump
articles.description: Former President Donald Trump has strongly criticized a recent United States Supreme Court decision regarding tariffs. He stated the ruling could lead to the country repaying companies approximately $159 billion. Trump believes a minor alteration in the court's wording could have prevented this significant financial outflow. This development impacts government revenue and trade relations.

articles.title: Axis Bank, IDFC First Bank, RBL Bank, India Cements Q4 Results Today - Earning Estimates
articles.description: Axis Bank's Net interest income (NII), a key gauge of lending performance, is pegged at Rs 14,727.48 crore, reflecting continued traction in credit growth and stable margins.

articles.title: Markets have begun to climb the wall of worry
articles.description: Markets often climb despite worries. In 2020, markets rose before economic recovery. Now, in 2026, markets are recovering amid geopolitical tensions. Investor behavior shows learning from past experiences. Capital is entering markets during fear. While risks remain, markets may have already priced in much of the uncertainty. Markets lead sentiment, adjusting prices before clarity emerges.

articles.title: FD rate up to 8% for senior citizens investing for three years; Know list of banks
articles.description: Senior citizens can still secure fixed deposit (FD) interest rates up to 8.05% for a three-year term from select small finance banks, with a cap of Rs 3 crore. Banks deduct TDS on FD interest exceeding Rs 1 lakh annually. Senior citizens with zero tax liability can submit Form 15H to avoid this deduction.

articles.title: K’taka approves revised internal reservation for SCs
articles.description: Karnataka approves sub-quotas for SCs, allocating 5.25% each for right-hand and left-hand Dalits, and 4.5% for other SC groups, maintaining a 15% total reservation.

articles.title: Capital One App Down: Bank Users Report 'We Hit A Snag' Error Message Amid Outage
articles.description: The Capital One app faced an outage with nearly 4,000 users reporting issues and seeing a “We hit a snag” error message. The company acknowledged a known display issue and advised customers to check back later or contact support for urgent needs.

articles.title: Economist Ashok Lahiri from Bengal to become Niti Aayog vice-chairman, Gobardhan Das named as member
articles.description: Economist Ashok Lahiri and scientist Gobardhan Das from West Bengal are set to join Niti Aayog. Lahiri, a former chief economic advisor, will be the vice-chairman. Das, director of IISER Bhopal, will serve as a member. This appointment brings distinguished expertise to the policy think tank. Their contributions are expected to shape future economic and scientific initiatives.

articles.title: When the acquisition looks great but the footnote doesn't: How Indian managements turn deals into balance-sheet fiction
articles.description: Acquisitions are among corporate finance’s most celebrated events. But the harder story usually emerges later, when the accounting begins to reflect what the deal actually delivered. This is not always about outright fraud. It is often about judgement. Used carefully, that judgement can reflect business reality. Used aggressively, it can make an ordinary deal look exceptional, a weak business appear stable, and a balance sheet seem stronger than it really is.

articles.title: Promoters Buying: 9 Nifty 500 stocks that saw stake hikes in March quarter
articles.description: Promoters are showing increased confidence in several Nifty 500 companies. Their shareholding has risen in the March 2026 quarter compared to the December 2025 quarter. This trend suggests optimism about future company performance. Investors may find this a positive indicator. Companies like Godrej Properties and Adani Energy Solutions are among those with increased promoter stakes.

articles.title: US stocks: Nuclear reactor maker X-Energy valued at $11.9 billion in stellar Nasdaq debut
articles.description: X-Energy's Nasdaq debut saw shares surge 30.9%, valuing the Amazon-backed nuclear reactor developer at $11.9 billion. The company raised over $1 billion in its IPO, aiming to deploy its small modular reactors to power AI infrastructure and meet carbon-free energy goals. This marks a significant capital milestone for X-Energy's ambitious plans.

articles.title: GST fault line: Supreme Court to rule if taxman can bundle years into one sweeping show-cause notice
articles.description: The Supreme Court is set to decide if tax authorities can issue a single show-cause notice for multiple financial years. This procedural issue impacts how tax exposure is assessed and litigated, with potential to aggregate liabilities and blur limitation boundaries. The court will determine if the CGST Act permits composite proceedings across years or mandates year-by-year liability initiation.

articles.title: China Pushes Yuan Global As Dollar Dominance Faces Challenge
articles.description: China is actively promoting the renminbi as a global currency to lessen its dependence on the US dollar, viewing this shift as crucial for national security.

articles.title: Actis picks Standard Chartered to kick off $2 billion sale of BluPine
articles.description: Actis is selling its Indian renewable energy firm BluPine Energy. The company has a capacity of 4 gigawatts. Standard Chartered Bank is advising on the sale. The price tag is $2 billion. This follows Actis's previous successful sales of renewable energy platforms in India. Deal activity in the sector remains steady as investors seek exits.

articles.title: Russia Explodes At Europe's €90 Bn 'Loan' For Zelensky; 'Enjoy Getting Played Again'
articles.description: Dmitry Medvedev has ignited a fresh controversy by blasting the European Union’s €90 billion loan package to Ukraine, claiming the burden will ultimately fall on ordinary Europeans while Brussels “fantasises” that Russia will foot the bill. In a fiery post on X, Medvedev mocked EU leaders and accused them of draining taxpayers’ pockets to bankroll Kyiv’s war effort with little hope of repayment.

articles.title: India growth expected to be around 6.5% this year: Gita Gopinath
articles.description: India's economy is projected to grow at 6.5% this year. Global risks persist, impacting supply chains and potentially leading to food price increases. Companies are absorbing costs, but this may not last. Central banks might delay rate cuts due to muted global demand. Any oil shock will affect many sectors.

articles.description: The Nifty traded below the 23,900 level. All the sectoral indices on the NSE were traded in the red, with IT, media and pharma shares declining the most.

articles.title: Pani puri vendor sells cart for wife's hospital bill, customer invests Rs 1.5 lakh for fresh start
articles.description: A pani puri vendor sold his cart to cover his wife's hospital bills, leaving him with nothing. One loyal customer tracked him down, skipped the sympathy, and handed him a Rs 1.5 lakh business plan.

articles.title: 'May not be sufficient': FM Sitharaman sounds alarm on AI threats to Indian banks
articles.description: Finance Minister flags that decades of strong cybersecurity may be insufficient against AI-driven threats, directs banks to work under an IBA-led mechanism to identify vulnerabilities and invest in new defences.
